sections.heading: Syntax
Options
sections.body:
`range` uses the following terms to construct a query:

| Field     | Type                                      | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Necessity |
| --------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------- |
| gt or gte | BSON date, number, or string, or objectId | Find values greater than (>) or greater than or equal to (>=) the given value.For number fields, the value can be an int32, int64, or double data type.For date fields, the value must be an ISODate formatted date.For string fields, the value must be indexed as MongoDB Search token type.For ObjectId fields, the value must be indexed as objectId data type or dynamic mappings must be set to true.                                                                                      | no        |
| lt or lte | BSON date, number, or string, or objectId | Find values less than (\<) or less than or equal to (\<=) the given value.For number fields, the value can be an int32, int64, or double data type.For date fields, the value must be an ISODate formatted date.For string fields, the value must be indexed as MongoDB Search token type.For ObjectId fields, the value must be indexed as objectId data type or dynamic mappings must be set to true.                                                                                          | no        |
| path      | string or array of strings                | Indexed field or fields to search.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | yes       |
| score     | object                                    | Modify the score assigned to matching search results. You can modify the default score using the following options:boost: multiply the result score by the given number.constant: replace the result score with the given number.function: replace the result score with the given expression.When querying array values, MongoDB Search assigns higher scores if more values in the array match the query.For information on using score in your query, see Score the Documents in the Results. | no        |

sections.heading: Examples
sections.body:
The following examples use the collection in the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets). If you loaded the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets) on your cluster, you can create the
indexes using the index definitions in the examples below and run the
example queries on your cluster.

sections.heading: Number Example
sections.body:
The following examples use indexes on numeric fields in the
[sample data sets](available-sample-datasets) and run
`range` queries against the indexed fields.

Example 1

sections.heading: Sample Index
Sample Query
Sample Index
Sample Query
Number Example
sections.body:
For this example, you can use either static or dynamic mappings to
index the `runtime` field in the `sample_mflix.movies`
collection. The query uses `gte` and `lte` fields to define
the numeric range to search.

sections.heading: Sample Index
Sample Query
Date Example
sections.body:
The following example uses the `range` operator to query a date
field in the `sample_mflix.movies` collection. For this example, you
can use either static or dynamic mappings to index the `date` type
field named `released` in the collection.

ObjectId Example
sections.body:
The following example uses the `range` operator to query an
`objectId` field in the `sample_mflix.movies` collection. For this
example, you can use either static or dynamic mappings to index the
`objectId` type field named `_id` in the collection.

sections.heading: Sample Index
sections.body:
The following index definition named `default` indexes all the
fields in the `movies` collection.

String Example
sections.body:
The following example uses the `range` operator to query a string
field in the `sample_mflix.movies` collection. For this example, you
must use static mappings to index the field named `title` in the
collection as MongoDB Search `token` type.
